title: chamaesyce orbiculata subsp. nilagirica
intro: Chamaesyce orbiculata subsp. nilagirica, also known as Nilagiri Spurge, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds in its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Nilagiri Spurge include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Nilagiri Spurge is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, such as creams or ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and promote healing.
